frameworks.name: hierarchical Bayesian model of pedagogy
frameworks.one_line: teacher and learner jointly infer each other's beliefs across nested levels

frameworks.name: computational accounts of pedagogy
frameworks.one_line: extends pedagogical sampling to interactive teacher-learner feedback loops

core_question: How do teachers infer learners' background knowledge, and how does learner feedback shape this collaborative teaching process?
key_assumptions: teaching and learning are collaborative inference over latent beliefs
learners can signal confusion and background knowledge to teachers
examples are selected to be informative given a model of the learner
manipulations: presence and content of learner feedback to the teacher
key_findings: teachers select examples conditioned on learner's background knowledge
teachers adjust subsequent examples in response to learner feedback
learners provide more feedback when examples mismatch their background knowledge
interpretations: pedagogy is a bidirectional inference process between teacher and learner
interactive feedback supports computational models of collaborative teaching
